reason: Both answers correctly say no, identify the deploy pipeline/CI as the real bottleneck, give concrete fixes (continuous deployment, faster CI, feature flags, canary/rollback), correctly note 15 engineers is far below microservices scale, cite Shopify/GitHub as monolith-at-scale precedent, and recommend modular monolith (Packwerk) as the next step if boundaries become painful. Both hit all four key points. A is more polished prose with clean formatting and no grammatical errors, and includes GitHub alongside Shopify. B is terser with occasional broken grammar but adds a bit more specificity (explicit team-size heuristic ~50+, a concrete revisit trigger). These trade-offs roughly cancel out; neither has a correctness error that would mislead the asker.
